response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior/staff-level individual contributor with genuinely relevant developer-tooling experience (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, device orchestration, MCP code generators) and some mobile-platform exposure (iOS/Apple TV apps). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of formal people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, running one-on-ones, owning team roadmaps, or leading organizational change. Community teaching and founding an open-source project suggest mentorship aptitude, but not the required management track record.
strengths: Deep hands-on developer-tools experience: test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/GitLab infrastructure, and device orchestration tooling
Broad technical range (web, mobile, embedded, low-latency systems) with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and mentorship through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience (required: 2+ years managing or leading a team)
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, performance expectations, or organizational roadmaps
Mobile experience is app-development focused, not mobile-platform build/test infrastructure at ecosystem scale

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure background
